summary: M.S. in Artificial Intelligence with experience designing, building, and deploying production AI agents and developer productivity tooling end-to-end. Built a multi-agent conversational platform with prompt engineering, RAG pipelines, and LLM orchestration in Python, plus automated CI/CD pipelines and a 35+ test evaluation suite for agent behavior. Strong fundamentals in data structures, algorithms, and distributed components (PostgreSQL, Pinecone, Docker, Kubernetes), with hands-on ownership of tools from prototype through monitoring and production support.

skills.languages: Python, Go (familiar), TypeScript, JavaScript, SQL, Java (familiar), C++ (familiar)
skills.ml: LLM Agent Architectures & Orchestration, Prompt Engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), LangChain, LLM APIs (Claude, GPT-4o), Vector Embeddings, Pinecone, HuggingFace Transformers, XGBoost
skills.infra: Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD (GitHub Actions), PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, Message Queues, FastAPI, Express.js, Node.js, RESTful APIs, AWS, GCP, Git

experience.title: Deep Learning Intern
bullets: Developed a YOLO-based object detection model in Python to detect and classify industrial pipe inventory from warehouse images, improving accuracy by 30% and eliminating a fully manual counting process.
Containerized the model with Docker and deployed on Kubernetes for horizontal scaling of model inference, enabling the team to handle variable load without code changes.

Built a data augmentation pipeline using NumPy and Pandas, scaling the training dataset from a few hundred to 10,000+ images and manually re-annotating 500+ edge cases; accuracy improved from 65% to 85.4% on held-out evaluation data.
Designed normalized relational database schemas in SQL to store image metadata and inference results, enabling cross-team queries on model performance and inventory trends.

projects.name: AI Interview Engine
projects.subtitle: Voice-First Candidate Screening Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Express, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, Deepgram, Supabase, Railway, Vercel
bullets: Architected and built a production multi-agent pipeline orchestrating Deepgram Nova-2 (speech-to-text), Claude Sonnet 4.6 (conversation/reasoning), Claude Haiku (document parsing), and Deepgram Aura (text-to-speech), achieving 4-5s end-to-end latency per turn and owning the system end-to-end from design through deployment and monitoring.

Designed a multi-turn agentic workflow with dynamic context injection: the agent ingests each candidate's parsed resume before the session and uses prompt engineering to generate targeted, role-specific follow-up questions in real time.

Built an automated document ingestion pipeline (PDF parsing, Claude Haiku, MD5 deduplication caching) and a post-interview evaluation agent producing structured advance/hold/reject decisions across 4 scoring dimensions; deployed full-stack on Railway, Vercel, and Supabase (PostgreSQL with Row-Level Security and Magic Link authentication) with Brevo SMTP for transactional email automation.

projects.name: CodeSyntax AI
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack RAG Developer Assistant
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, GitHub Actions
bullets: Built a production retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) developer tool using GPT-4o-mini with semantic search over Python Docs, RealPython, and StackOverflow, indexed in Pinecone via OpenAI vector embeddings to reduce developer context-switching.

Implemented an LLM-based query intent classifier and result re-ranker by source authority, plus multi-turn conversation support via LangChain chat-history condensing, both measurably reducing off-topic responses.

Maintained 35+ pytest unit tests and a 55-question domain evaluation set; built a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline that builds the Docker image and deploys to Railway (API) and Vercel (frontend) on every merge to main, providing ongoing operational support for the tool.

projects.name: AuthentiText
projects.subtitle: AI-Generated Text Detection Engine
projects.stack: Python, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, GCP, Docker
bullets: Trained a weighted ensemble model combining XGBoost (21 hand-crafted linguistic features: perplexity, burstiness, lexical density) with a fine-tuned DistilBERT transformer, achieving 99.1% F1 and 99.96% AUROC on held-out test data.

Conducted systematic model comparison across four architectures (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, DistilBERT) using ROC curves, confusion matrices, and SHAP feature importance; integrated Captum token-level attribution heatmaps for explainable AI (XAI).
Trained on the merged HC3 corpus spanning Wikipedia, Reddit, medicine, and finance domains; deployed as a publicly accessible machine learning inference app on HuggingFace Spaces.
